items.summary: This conversation with Adam Marchick contrasts the LP conversations that happen most often with those that actually move the needle on fund decisions. It offers GPs practical guidance on prioritizing which LP relationships and topics deserve the most attention.

items.title: Google Part III: The AI Company
items.summary: The concluding chapter of the Google series examining its transformation into an AI-first company, covering DeepMind, Gemini, and the competitive dynamics reshaping search and cloud. Important for understanding how a legacy tech giant is repositioning capital and talent around frontier AI.

items.title: Acquired Live at Radio City Music Hall (Presented by J.P. Morgan)
items.summary: A live show recorded at Radio City Music Hall, sponsored by J.P. Morgan, likely blending audience engagement with broader reflections on business and markets. Signals Acquired's growing cultural reach and its ties to major financial institutions.

items.title: NO Diligence is Ever Enough | Anurag Chandra | Superclusters | S6E2
items.summary: Anurag Chandra makes the case that due diligence in venture is inherently incomplete, pushing LPs and GPs to rethink how much verification is truly achievable before backing a fund or founder. The conversation likely explores frameworks for managing residual risk rather than chasing false certainty.

items.title: NVIDIA: OpenAI, Future of Compute, and the American Dream | BG2 w/ Bill Gurley and Brad Gerstner
How to Not Get Fired When Changing Your VC Strategy | El Pack w/ Beezer Clarkson | Superclusters
items.summary: Beezer Clarkson joins the El Pack roundtable to discuss how emerging and established GPs can pivot their investment strategy without alarming existing LPs. Expect tactical advice on communication timing and framing strategy shifts as evolution rather than risk.

items.title: Fundraising as a Fund of Funds with Alex Edelson
How to Read Investors Like a Book | Thorsten Claus | Superclusters | S6E1
items.summary: Thorsten Claus shares heuristics for assessing investor psychology and behavior, helping fund managers better negotiate with and select LPs. The episode offers a people-reading toolkit relevant to fundraising and syndicate-building.

items.title: EP 150: Bret Taylor (CEO, Sierra): A New Class of Software Winners
Inside OpenAI Enterprise: Forward Deployed Engineering, GPT-5, and More | BG2 Guest Interview
EP 149: Marc Benioff (CEO, Salesforce) Predicts Half of Conversations Will be With AI Agents Next Year
China, China, China. Breaking Down China’s Tech Surge | BG2 w/ Bill Gurley and Brad Gerstner
Google Part II: Alphabet
items.summary: This installment covers Google's restructuring into Alphabet, examining the rationale behind the holding-company model and its implications for capital allocation across bets like Waymo and Verily. Useful for GPs thinking about corporate structure as a tool for managing diverse innovation portfolios.

items.title: Minisode: Curiosity, Chemistry, and Keeping Relevance Across the Partnership
Backing Outliers in Venture with Sunil Dhaliwal & Mike Dauber
How to Increase Dialogue with your LPs | El Pack w/ Kelli Fontaine | Superclusters
items.summary: Kelli Fontaine discusses practical approaches GPs can use to deepen and sustain communication with their LP base beyond quarterly updates. The talk centers on building trust and transparency that pays off during future fundraises.

items.title: 81% of America is Underfunded | Vijen Patel & Grady Buchanan | Superclusters | S5PSE1
items.summary: Vijen Patel and Grady Buchanan examine capital access gaps across the U.S., arguing that the vast majority of the country remains underserved by venture funding. The discussion highlights opportunities for LPs seeking geographic diversification beyond coastal hubs.

items.title: China Open-Source, Compute Arms Race, Reordering Global Trade | BG2 w/ Bill Gurley and Brad Gerstner
How Many Exceptions Are Too Many? | El Pack w/ John Felix | Superclusters
items.summary: John Felix and the El Pack panel debate how much flexibility GPs should build into their investment thesis before exceptions undermine LP confidence. It's a candid look at maintaining fund discipline while staying opportunistic.

items.title: Minisode: Are Major Incumbents Finally at Risk in the Age of AI?
Uncompensated Risks in VC | Wendy Li | Superclusters | S5E12
items.summary: Wendy Li unpacks the concept of uncompensated risk in venture portfolios, identifying exposures LPs take on without corresponding return premiums. The episode is useful for LPs refining portfolio construction and risk-adjusted return expectations.

items.title: The Jamie Dimon Interview
items.summary: An interview with JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon covering his views on banking, risk management, and the broader economy. Offers LPs a rare window into the thinking of one of the most influential figures in global finance.

items.title: The Inside Peek Into How Family Offices Gather | Samira Salman | Superclusters | S5E11
items.summary: Samira Salman offers a rare look into how family offices convene, network, and evaluate opportunities, shedding light on this often opaque LP segment. GPs courting family office capital will find insight into their decision-making culture.
